generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-torque permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot, providing precise motion control for manufacturing applications."
]

instance CycloidalGearTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision cycloidal gear mechanism for smooth motion conversion and torque amplification in the robot's first axis drive system."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance FirstAxisPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder deployed per drive to measure real-time positional data of the first axis with high accuracy for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData FirstAxisPositionData
]

instance SecondAxisPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder deployed per drive to measure real-time positional data of the second axis with high accuracy for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondAxisPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance FirstAxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisPositionEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time positional data transmitted from the first axis position encoder via MQTT protocol for digital shadow aggregation."
]

instance SecondAxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondAxisPositionEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time positional data transmitted from the second axis position encoder via MQTT protocol for digital shadow aggregation."
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Contextual measurement data including input current, output angle, torque, and angular velocity transmitted from physical sensors to the digital representation."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing the functional/logical architecture of the robot's first axis components and their connector relationships."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL component-based model defining interfaces and connectors for the robot's first axis mechanical and electrical components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLModelingEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc state-based automaton model representing the dynamic behavior and state transitions of the first axis drive system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed behavioral simulation of the first axis dynamics including friction analysis and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance PositionalDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted positional data traces from the physical system's encoders, enabling contextual analysis for friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:fromData FirstAxisPositionData, SecondAxisPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance ContextualMeasurementTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured contextual measurement data including torque, angular velocity, and operational logs aggregated from physical sensors for diagnostic purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based MBSE platform enabling integrated modeling of functional and logical architecture across heterogeneous tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables FunctionalArchitectureService, LogicalArchitectureService
]

instance AADLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL-based architecture description tool supporting component-based modeling with interfaces and connectors for system decomposition."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentBasedModelingService
]

instance MontiArcEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework supporting component-based modeling and state-based behavior simulation for system dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine for detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic system analysis of the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicSimulationService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software module implementing passive data aggregation and abstraction of contextual measurement traces for digital shadow purposes."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, ContextualAnalysisService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive service aggregating real-time positional and contextual measurement data from physical sensors into structured digital traces."
    DTDFVocab:provides PositionalDataInsight, ContextualMeasurementInsight
]

instance ContextualAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling contextual abstraction of raw sensor data into structured traces for friction analysis and diagnostic purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML-based functional architecture modeling and decomposition of the robot's first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides FunctionalDesignInsight
]

instance LogicalArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling logical architecture modeling through SysML Internal Block Diagrams for component-connector relationships."
    DTDFVocab:provides LogicalDesignInsight
]

instance ComponentBasedModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting AADL-based component-based modeling with interfaces and connectors for system decomposition."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentModelingInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling MontiArc state-based behavioral simulation of the first axis drive system dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralModelingInsight
]

instance DynamicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing Simulink-based dynamic simulation of the first axis including friction modeling and control logic validation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance PositionalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight derived from aggregated positional data traces enabling real-time monitoring of axis positioning accuracy."
]

instance ContextualMeasurementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight generated from contextual measurement traces supporting diagnostic analysis of operational parameters like torque and angular velocity."
]

instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling friction identification through analysis of positional and contextual measurement data traces."
]

instance FunctionalDesignInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing functional architecture modeling results for the robot's first axis components and their relationships."
]

instance LogicalDesignInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight derived from logical architecture modeling supporting component-connector analysis of the first axis system."
]

instance ComponentModelingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from AADL-based component modeling enabling interface and connector analysis of the first axis components."
]

instance BehavioralModelingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from MontiArc state-based behavioral modeling representing system dynamics and state transitions."
]

instance SimulationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from Simulink-based dynamic simulation validating the first axis friction model and control logic under various operational conditions."
]

// C12: Environment
instance ManufacturingRobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot in precision manufacturing applications."
    base:contains FirstAxisComponentEnvironment
]

instance FirstAxisComponentEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment containing the first axis components including motor, gear transmission, and sensing elements."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, CycloidalGearTransmission, FirstAxisPositionEncoder, SecondAxisPositionEncoder
]

// C13: Components
instance FirstAxisComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Primary structural component of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot's first axis responsible for motion control in manufacturing applications."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisComponentEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the described Digital Twin system is **not explicitly detailed** in the provided documents, as the focus is primarily on **digital shadows** (unidirectional data flow) and **modeling methodologies** (e.g., MBSE, ADLs). However, the broader context implies that a Digital Twin **can** provide active components to **interpret and process** incoming data and potentially **push commands back** to physical systems [35–37].

**Inferred Components (if applicable):**
- **Control flows** would likely involve **command validation**, **authentication**, and **feedback mechanisms** (e.g., confirmation of command execution).
- **Conflict resolution** may rely on **rule-based systems** or **simulation-based validation** (e.g., using **SysML state machines** [17] or **MontiArc Automaton** [25]).
- **Feedback systems** could include **real-time monitoring** of physical responses (e.g., via MQTT [52] or IoT platforms [53]) to adjust virtual models dynamically.

**Technical Gaps:**
- **No explicit mention** of command types (e.g., REST APIs, OPC UA), message formats (e.g., JSON, XML), or **authentication protocols** (e.g., OAuth, TLS) in the documents.
- **No quantification** of latency, reliability, or **command execution confirmation** mechanisms.

**Conclusion:**
Since the documents **do not provide specific details** on bidirectional control flows, this characteristic is **not explicitly supported** by the provided context. For a comprehensive DT system, such interactions would typically involve **IoT gateways**, **edge computing**, and **real-time middleware** (e.g., ThingsBoard [53]), but these are **not described** here.
Not in Document
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents operates within a **real-time, data-driven** temporal framework, where data traces are collected and processed as **automated one-way flows** from the physical system to the digital representation. The system emphasizes **fine-grained traceability** of data traces, enabling transitive tracking of their origins (e.g., processing components, input traces, or models) via **association links** (e.g., 'originates from' relationships). While the documents do not specify explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., millisecond-level latency targets or sampling intervals), the **streaming-based data collection** (implied by MQTT usage in horizontal integration) suggests low-latency requirements for maintaining temporal consistency. No explicit time scales (e.g., microsecond, millisecond, or second granularity) or orchestration of multi-timescale services (e.g., real-time control vs. predictive analytics) are detailed in the provided context."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is orchestrated through a **reference model-based approach**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** defines foundational concepts (e.g., data traces, processing components, metadata) that are **concretized** into a **conforming model** for specific use cases [32, 41]. This involves **architecture patterns** derived from **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., SysML [17], ADLs like AADL [24], or MontiArc [25, 26]), which describe systems via **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., interfaces, interconnections, composition). The constellation integrates **heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives** [31] by combining models from different notations (e.g., SysML IBDs, state machines, or simulation tools like Simulink [28]). Resource management is implied through the **processing logic** embedded in data calculation or simulation models, which operate on input data traces to produce output data. System-wide coordination is achieved via **traceability links** (e.g., transitive associations) that map data traces to their origins (processing components, models) and ensure **fine-grained traceability** [32]. No explicit mention of orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or resource allocation strategies is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define component-connector perspectives, interfaces, and interconnections. Behavior modeling at the logical and product layers integrates **state machines (SysML v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or external simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java). Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool interoperability**, addressing complexity through modularized modeling approaches like **RFLP (Requirements-Functions-Logics-Physics), OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are referenced in the document, but the emphasis on **abstraction, toolchain heterogeneity, and stakeholder alignment** implies iterative refinement phases from conceptual design to operational deployment."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity in the Digital Twin system is evaluated through **comparative analysis of simulated vs. measured data**, where deviations between model predictions and real-world sensor inputs (e.g., encoder readings) are analyzed to assess model accuracy. However, the documents do not explicitly outline **formal Verification & Validation (V&V) methodologies**, **uncertainty quantification frameworks**, or **error correction mechanisms**. Instead, fidelity is implicitly addressed via **cross-model consistency checks** required when integrating heterogeneous notations (e.g., SysML, Simulink) within the MBSE framework. Quality assurance processes are not detailed beyond the need to 'cope with models in different notations and tools'"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection for this Digital Twin system is limited to the **MQTT protocol** for streaming sensor data from physical assets to the digital shadow, as referenced in [52]. No explicit details are provided regarding network topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols for network communication. The system’s passive nature (as a digital shadow) implies no active control or feedback loops, thus no bidirectional communication protocols (e.g., REST APIs, WebSockets, or proprietary protocols) are mentioned. The only technical connection explicitly noted is the **unidirectional data flow via MQTT**, with no further elaboration on infrastructure or communication architecture."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems via **MQTT-based streaming protocols** for data exchange, as implied by the context of digital shadows in manufacturing. While the documents do not explicitly mention **OPC UA** or other protocols, the use of MQTT suggests adherence to **lightweight, publish-subscribe interoperability standards** for real-time data flow. The system references **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** and **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** within the Digital Shadow Reference Model, but these are framed as internal architectural concepts rather than horizontal integration with external enterprise systems. No details are provided on **API gateways**, **service-oriented architectures (SOA)**, or **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., ESBs). The scope of horizontal integration is limited to the **data traces and processing components** defined for the specific use case, with no explicit dependencies on external systems beyond the streaming infrastructure."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates a **safety cell** mechanism to restrict the operational working range of robotic assets, ensuring physical safety by confining movement within predefined boundaries. Sensor data, including time series from all sensors, is transmitted via **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**, a lightweight protocol designed for IoT and real-time data streaming. While the documents do not explicitly detail encryption protocols or authentication systems, the use of MQTT implies reliance on underlying transport security measures (e.g., TLS) for data integrity and confidentiality during transmission. No explicit mention of **RAMI 4.0 cybersecurity layers**, **blockchain audit logs**, **IEC 61508 compliance**, or **federated learning** is present in the provided context. The system’s safety considerations are primarily hardware-based (safety cell) rather than software-based protocols or regulatory frameworks."
]
